rationale: Both evaluations land on the same score (4/10) and rest on the same substantive reasoning: minimal and dated C++ exposure (~6-7 months at National Instruments in 2014-2015), no IEC 62443/SDL industrial cybersecurity experience, and no exposure to MQTT/Modbus/OPC UA industrial protocols. The wording differs (13+ vs 10+ years, ~6 vs ~7 months) but these are run-to-run rewording variations well within ordinary noise, and none of the variant's reasoning keys off the country change. The location swap from various cities to 'United States' produced no new concern, no score shift, and no demographic-driven reasoning.
